FrameworkFeedbackResources

The clearing intelligence

Market optimization

Market optimization defines how bids and constraints become a clearing result: through cost minimization, welfare, feasibility, OPF, sensitivities, or simplified algorithms.

Return to feedback section

Conceptual role

This pillar evaluates whether optimization is centralized, sequential, simultaneous, distributed, or approximated. It connects mathematical design to data availability and market transparency.

The detailed page is intended to help reviewers examine the assumptions behind this pillar before providing targeted feedback.

Questions for reviewers

Is the clearing method implementable with available data? Is it transparent enough? Does it include the right network and market constraints?

  • Are the current features sufficient to describe real European market arrangements?
  • Are there missing options from demonstrators, regulation, or operational practice?
  • Does the terminology work across research, regulation, and implementation?

Connection to the platform

This pillar can be connected to dashboards, data exchange, market-clearing tools, and feedback workflows. Its purpose is to make design assumptions explicit before they become software, regulation, or market rules.

Back to framework
